main_paper.raw: {"methods":["A novel approach for novel view synthesis that integrates pretrained 3D priors (from Zero123++) into a 3D-free, HawkI-style optimization framework during inference","Use of off-the-shelf pretrained models (Zero123++, Stable Diffusion) in a plug-and-play manner","Incorporation of 3D prior images as pseudo or weak guidance images to provide camera angle control","Introduction of a regularization loss term to align target angle embedding (azimuth and elevation) with the optimized embedding in CLIP space"],"problems":["Achieving novel view synthesis from a single image without requiring multi-view or 3D data","Enabling precise camera control (azimuth and elevation) in 3D-free novel view synthesis methods","Improving viewpoint consistency and methods: A novel approach for novel view synthesis that integrates pretrained 3D priors (from Zero123++) into a 3D-free, HawkI-style optimization framework during inference
Use of off-the-shelf pretrained models (Zero123++, Stable Diffusion) in a plug-and-play manner
Incorporation of 3D prior images as pseudo or weak guidance images to provide camera angle control
Introduction of a regularization loss term to align target angle embedding (azimuth and elevation) with the optimized embedding in CLIP space
problems: Achieving novel view synthesis from a single image without requiring multi-view or 3D data
Enabling precise camera control (azimuth and elevation) in 3D-free novel view synthesis methods
Improving viewpoint consistency and fidelity in generated images, especially for complex scenes with multiple objects and detailed backgrounds
Overcoming the limitations of CLIP and 3D-free methods in understanding and controlling specific camera angles
metrics: Fidelity with respect to input image

results.metric: LPIPS gap of Zero123++ over lowest-performing model (from Shi et al. 2023a)

novelty_claims: First approach to combine pretrained 3D priors with 3D-free, diffusion-based novel view synthesis for precise camera control from a single image, without requiring 3D or multi-view data for training or inference
Analysis and demonstration of the limitations of CLIP in understanding specific 3D angles, motivating the use of 3D priors for camera control
Introduction of a regularization loss to align angle embeddings in CLIP space, improving viewpoint consistency
Demonstrated significant improvements over both 3D-based and 3D-free baselines in text consistency and fidelity, especially for complex, real-world images

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["NeRDi: Single-view NeRF synthesis framework using 2D diffusion models as general image priors","Optimization of NeRF representations by minimizing diffusion loss on arbitrary view renderings with a pretrained image diffusion model under input-view constraint","Two-section language guidance as conditioning input to the diffusion model (image caption and text embedding via textual inversion)","Geometric loss based on estimated depth maps for 3D geometry regularization"],"problems":["Single-view 3D reconstruction (NeRF synthesis) from a single 2D image without 3D supervision","Improving multiview content coherence in novel view synthesis from a single image","Generalizing NeRF synthesis to in-the-wild images without requiring matched multiview images or calibrated camera poses"],"datasets":["DTU MVS"],"metrics":["Novel view synthesis quality (likely PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, though not explicitly stated in the excerpt)"],"results":[{"metric":"Novel view synthesis quality on DTU MVS","value":"Higher quality than existing supervised baselines (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to formulate single-view NeRF synthesis as a conditioned 3D generation problem using 2D diffusion model priors without 3D supervision","Introduction of a two-section semantic guidance (image caption and text embedding) to improve semantic and visual coherence in novel view synthesis","Incorporation of a geometric regularization term based on estimated depth maps to regularize 3D geometry","Demonstration of zero-shot NeRF synthesis capability for in-the-wild images"]}

methods: NeRDi: Single-view NeRF synthesis framework using 2D diffusion models as general image priors
Optimization of NeRF representations by minimizing diffusion loss on arbitrary view renderings with a pretrained image diffusion model under input-view constraint
Two-section language guidance as conditioning input to the diffusion model (image caption and text embedding via textual inversion)
Geometric loss based on estimated depth maps for 3D geometry regularization
problems: Single-view 3D reconstruction (NeRF synthesis) from a single 2D image without 3D supervision
Improving multiview content coherence in novel view synthesis from a single image
Generalizing NeRF synthesis to in-the-wild images without requiring matched multiview images or calibrated camera poses

results.metric: Novel view synthesis quality on DTU MVS
results.value: Higher quality than existing supervised baselines (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: First to formulate single-view NeRF synthesis as a conditioned 3D generation problem using 2D diffusion model priors without 3D supervision
Introduction of a two-section semantic guidance (image caption and text embedding) to improve semantic and visual coherence in novel view synthesis
Incorporation of a geometric regularization term based on estimated depth maps to regularize 3D geometry
Demonstration of zero-shot NeRF synthesis capability for in-the-wild images

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Zero-1-to-3 framework: a conditional diffusion model for novel view synthesis from a single image","Learning camera viewpoint controls by fine-tuning large diffusion models (e.g., Stable Diffusion) on synthetic datasets","Viewpoint-conditioned diffusion for 3D reconstruction from a single image"],"problems":["Novel view synthesis from a single RGB image (changing camera viewpoint given one image)","3D object reconstruction from a single image in an under-constrained, open-world setting","Generalization to out-of-distribution and in-the-wild images, including non-photorealistic images"],"datasets":["Synthetic dataset for training viewpoint controls","CO3D (referenced as a benchmark for comparison)","In-the-wild images, including impressionist paintings (for qualitative evaluation)"],"metrics":["Quantitative evaluation metrics for novel view synthesis (e.g., image similarity metrics such as PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, though not explicitly named in the provided text)","Quantitative evaluation metrics for 3D reconstruction (e.g., geometry and appearance accuracy, though not explicitly named)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to demonstrate that large diffusion models (trained only on 2D images) have learned rich 3D priors enabling zero-shot novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from a single image","Propose a method to control camera viewpoint in large diffusion models via fine-tuning, enabling generation of new views of an object from a single image","Achieve state-of-the-art results for zero-shot novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction from a single RGB image, outperforming previous single-view methods","Demonstrate strong zero-shot generalization to out-of-distribution datasets and in-the-wild images, including artistic paintings"]}

Demonstrate strong zero-shot generalization to out-of-distribution datasets and in-the-wild images, including artistic paintings

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Feed-forward single image to 3D mesh pipeline","View-conditioned 2D diffusion model (Zero123) for multi-view image synthesis","Cost-volume-based neural surface reconstruction (SparseNeuS, a variant of MVSNeRF)","Elevation estimation module for camera pose estimation","Series of training strategies for handling inconsistent multi-view predictions"],"problems":["Single image 3D reconstruction (from a single 2D image to a 3D mesh)","Reducing lengthy per-shape optimization time in 3D reconstruction","Improving 3D consistency and geometry quality in reconstructed meshes","Generalizing 3D reconstruction to arbitrary object categories without class-specific priors","Handling inconsistent multi-view predictions from 2D diffusion models"],"datasets":["Synthetic data (unspecified)","In-the-wild images (real images, unspecified sources)"],"metrics":["Mesh quality (visual and geometric fidelity)","Runtime (seconds to reconstruct a mesh)"],"results":[{"metric":"Runtime","value":"45 seconds per 3D mesh reconstruction"},{"metric":"Mesh quality","value":"Outperforms existing methods (qualitative and quantitative, details not specified)"}],"novelty_claims":["First feed-forward method to reconstruct a full 360-degree 3D textured mesh from a single image in 45 seconds without per-shape optimization.","Combines a 2D diffusion model (Zero123) with a cost-volume-based 3D reconstruction technique for efficient and high-quality mesh generation.","Introduces essential training strategies to enable 360-degree mesh reconstruction from inconsistent multi-view predictions.","Proposes an elevation estimation module to estimate camera pose from a single image for 3D reconstruction.","Demonstrates superior mesh quality and efficiency compared to existing optimization-based methods."]}

novelty_claims: First feed-forward method to reconstruct a full 360-degree 3D textured mesh from a single image in 45 seconds without per-shape optimization.

Introduces essential training strategies to enable 360-degree mesh reconstruction from inconsistent multi-view predictions.
Proposes an elevation estimation module to estimate camera pose from a single image for 3D reconstruction.
Demonstrates superior mesh quality and efficiency compared to existing optimization-based methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Viewpoint Neural Textual Inversion (ViewNeTI): a method that learns a neural mapping from camera viewpoint parameters to text encoder latents (view tokens) to control 3D viewpoint in images generated by frozen 2D diffusion models via textual inversion"],"problems":["Controlling the 3D viewpoint of objects in images generated by pretrained 2D diffusion models","Novel View Synthesis (NVS) from very few input views, including single-view NVS","Investigating whether 2D diffusion models encode 3D scene representations and how to exploit them for 3D vision tasks"],"datasets":["DTU dataset"],"metrics":["LPIPS (Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity)"],"results":[{"metric":"LPIPS on DTU","value":"state-of-the-art (exact value not specified in provided text)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to show that textual inversion can be used to learn a 3D control token for viewpoint in frozen diffusion models","Demonstrates that 2D diffusion models like Stable Diffusion encode 3D scene representations that can be controlled via learned tokens in the text embedding space","Proposes a method (ViewNeTI) that enables 3D viewpoint control and novel view synthesis from very few input images, including single-view NVS, without modifying the base diffusion model","Shows evidence for both scene-specific and general (scene-disentangled) view-control manifolds in the text embedding space of diffusion models"]}

methods: Viewpoint Neural Textual Inversion (ViewNeTI): a method that learns a neural mapping from camera viewpoint parameters to text encoder latents (view tokens) to control 3D viewpoint in images generated by frozen 2D diffusion models via textual inversion
problems: Controlling the 3D viewpoint of objects in images generated by pretrained 2D diffusion models
Novel View Synthesis (NVS) from very few input views, including single-view NVS
Investigating whether 2D diffusion models encode 3D scene representations and how to exploit them for 3D vision tasks

results.value: state-of-the-art (exact value not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: First to show that textual inversion can be used to learn a 3D control token for viewpoint in frozen diffusion models
Demonstrates that 2D diffusion models like Stable Diffusion encode 3D scene representations that can be controlled via learned tokens in the text embedding space
Proposes a method (ViewNeTI) that enables 3D viewpoint control and novel view synthesis from very few input images, including single-view NVS, without modifying the base diffusion model
Shows evidence for both scene-specific and general (scene-disentangled) view-control manifolds in the text embedding space of diffusion models

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["TOSS: a text-guided novel view synthesis (NVS) model based on fine-tuning Stable Diffusion","Dense cross attention module for conditioning on both input image features and camera pose encoding","Specialized training strategy with two expert diffusion models: one for pose correctness, one for detail preservation","Image and camera pose conditioning modules tailored for text-guided NVS","Utilization of textual inversion and higher-capability text-to-image models for further improvements"],"problems":["Novel view synthesis (NVS) from a single RGB image is under-constrained and often produces implausible results","Lack of explicit user control in single-view NVS","Existing methods (e.g., Zero-1-to-3) treat NVS as image-to-image translation, leading to shape and texture deformations and implausible semantic meaning","Need for more controllable and plausible NVS results"],"datasets":["Objaverse"],"metrics":["Plausibility (qualitative and/or user study)","Controllability (qualitative and/or user study)","Multiview-consistency (qualitative and/or user study)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to introduce explicit text guidance to constrain the solution space in single-image NVS, enabling more plausible and controllable results","Proposes novel image and camera pose conditioning modules for text-guided NVS","Specialized training with expert diffusion models for pose correctness and detail preservation","Demonstrates that text guidance significantly improves NVS quality and controllability over prior methods like Zero-1-to-3","Approach is orthogonal to and compatible with advances in text-to-image generation, such as textual inversion"]}

methods: TOSS: a text-guided novel view synthesis (NVS) model based on fine-tuning Stable Diffusion
Dense cross attention module for conditioning on both input image features and camera pose encoding
Specialized training strategy with two expert diffusion models: one for pose correctness, one for detail preservation
Image and camera pose conditioning modules tailored for text-guided NVS
Utilization of textual inversion and higher-capability text-to-image models for further improvements
problems: Novel view synthesis (NVS) from a single RGB image is under-constrained and often produces implausible results

novelty_claims: First to introduce explicit text guidance to constrain the solution space in single-image NVS, enabling more plausible and controllable results
Proposes novel image and camera pose conditioning modules for text-guided NVS
Specialized training with expert diffusion models for pose correctness and detail preservation
Demonstrates that text guidance significantly improves NVS quality and controllability over prior methods like Zero-1-to-3

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["MVSNeRF: a neural rendering approach combining plane-swept cost volumes (from multi-view stereo) with physically based volume rendering for radiance field reconstruction","Use of a generic deep neural network to reconstruct radiance fields from only three input views via fast network inference","Construction of a cost volume at the reference view by warping 2D image features from nearby views onto sweeping planes","3D CNN to reconstruct a neural scene encoding volume from the cost volume","Multi-layer perceptron (MLP) to decode volume density and radiance at arbitrary continuous locations using trilinearly interpolated neural features","Differentiable ray marching for final rendering"],"problems":["Efficient and generalizable neural radiance field reconstruction for novel view synthesis","Reducing the need for expensive and time-consuming per-scene optimization in neural radiance field methods","Generalizing radiance field reconstruction to unseen scenes with only a few input images"],"datasets":["DTU dataset (for training and testing)","Other datasets with different scene distributions (not specifically named, but used for testing generalization)"],"metrics":["Rendering quality (e.g., photo-realism, likely measured by PSNR/SSIM, though not explicitly stated in the excerpt)","Optimization time"],"results":[{"metric":"Number of input images required for high-quality synthesis","value":"3"},{"metric":"Optimization time compared to NeRF","value":"Substantially less (minutes vs. hours)"},{"metric":"Rendering quality compared to NeRF and concurrent generalizable NeRF works","value":"Significantly outperforms with only three input images; Estimated radiance field can be further fine-tuned with dense images for even higher quality and faster optimization than NeRF
Enables practical and efficient neural scene reconstruction and rendering

methods: Two-stage coarse-to-fine optimization framework for text-to-3D synthesis
Stage 1: Coarse model optimization using low-resolution diffusion prior and sparse 3D hash grid structure
Stage 2: High-resolution mesh optimization using efficient differentiable renderer and high-resolution latent diffusion model
Utilization of multiple diffusion priors at different resolutions
Extension of text-to-image editing techniques to 3D object editing
problems: Slow optimization and low-resolution output in text-to-3D synthesis (as in DreamFusion)
Low-quality 3D models due to low-resolution image supervision
High computational and memory requirements for high-resolution 3D synthesis

novelty_claims: Proposes Magic3D, a high-quality text-to-3D synthesis framework with a coarse-to-fine strategy leveraging both low- and high-resolution diffusion priors.
Achieves 8× higher resolution supervision and 2× faster synthesis compared to DreamFusion.
Introduces a memory- and compute-efficient hash grid scene representation for the coarse stage.
Switches to mesh optimization with high-resolution diffusion priors and efficient differentiable rasterization for the fine stage.
Extends text-to-image editing techniques to 3D object editing, enabling creative controls over 3D synthesis.

methods: Magic123: a two-stage coarse-to-fine image-to-3D pipeline using both 2D and 3D diffusion priors
Stage 1: Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) optimization for coarse geometry
Stage 2: Memory-efficient differentiable mesh representation (Deep Marching Tetrahedra, DMTet) for high-resolution mesh and texture refinement
Reference view supervision and novel view synthesis guided by 2D and 3D priors
Single trade-off parameter to balance 2D and 3D priors for geometry exploration vs. exploitation
Textual inversion for consistent appearance across views
Monocular depth regularization to prevent degenerate solutions
problems: High-quality 3D mesh and texture generation from a single unposed image in the wild
Balancing imaginative geometry (exploration) and precise geometry (exploitation) in 3D reconstruction
Overcoming limitations of using only 2D or only 3D priors for image-to-3D generation
Memory and resolution constraints in neural 3D representations
datasets: Synthetic benchmarks (not specifically named in the provided text)
metrics: 3D reconstruction quality (implied, but specific metrics not named in the provided text)
novelty_claims: First to propose a two-stage coarse-to-fine image-to-3D pipeline using both 2D and 3D diffusion priors for high-quality mesh and texture generation from a single image
Introduction of a single trade-off parameter to control the balance between 2D and 3D priors, enabling user control over geometry exploration and exploitation
Achieves state-of-the-art results in single-image 3D reconstruction on both real-world and synthetic data using the same set of parameters without reconfiguration
Employs a memory-efficient mesh representation (DMTet) to enable high-resolution (up to 1K) mesh and texture refinement
Simultaneous use of 2D and 3D priors for reference and novel view supervision in both stages of the pipeline

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Image-conditioned diffusion model (Zero123++) for multi-view image generation","Tiling six views into a single image to model joint distribution for consistency","Finetuning from pretrained 2D diffusion models (e.g., Stable Diffusion) with improved conditioning schemes","Implementing various conditioning techniques (global and local) to maximize use of Stable Diffusion priors","Training at higher (native) resolution with strategies to address instability","Training a ControlNet on Zero123++ for enhanced control"],"problems":["Generating 3D-consistent multi-view images from a single input image","Overcoming texture degradation and geometric misalignment in multi-view generation","Improving consistency between generated views in diffusion-based novel view synthesis","Maximizing utilization of pretrained 2D generative priors in 3D content generation","Addressing instability and quality loss when training at higher resolutions"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes Zero123++, a new image-conditioned diffusion model for consistent multi-view generation from a single image","Introduces a tiling strategy to jointly model multiple views for improved consistency","Develops conditioning and training schemes to better leverage pretrained 2D diffusion models (Stable Diffusion)","Demonstrates training at higher resolution with strategies to address instability, improving image quality","Showcases the feasibility of training a ControlNet on Zero123++ for enhanced generation control"]}

methods: Image-conditioned diffusion model (Zero123++) for multi-view image generation
Tiling six views into a single image to model joint distribution for consistency
Finetuning from pretrained 2D diffusion models (e.g., Stable Diffusion) with improved conditioning schemes
Implementing various conditioning techniques (global and local) to maximize use of Stable Diffusion priors
Training at higher (native) resolution with strategies to address instability
Training a ControlNet on Zero123++ for enhanced control
problems: Generating 3D-consistent multi-view images from a single input image
Overcoming texture degradation and geometric misalignment in multi-view generation
Improving consistency between generated views in diffusion-based novel view synthesis
Maximizing utilization of pretrained 2D generative priors in 3D content generation
Addressing instability and quality loss when training at higher resolutions
novelty_claims: Proposes Zero123++, a new image-conditioned diffusion model for consistent multi-view generation from a single image
Introduces a tiling strategy to jointly model multiple views for improved consistency
Develops conditioning and training schemes to better leverage pretrained 2D diffusion models (Stable Diffusion)
Demonstrates training at higher resolution with strategies to address instability, improving image quality
Showcases the feasibility of training a ControlNet on Zero123++ for enhanced generation control

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Introducing explicit 3D shape priors into CLIP-guided 3D optimization for text-to-3D synthesis","Two-stage pipeline: (1) text-to-shape generation using a text-to-image diffusion model (Stable Diffusion) followed by an image-to-shape generator, (2) using the generated 3D shape as initialization for neural radiance field optimization with the full text prompt","Joint optimization of a learnable text prompt and fine-tuning of the text-to-image diffusion model to generate rendering-style images and reduce the style domain gap"],"problems":["Zero-shot text-to-3D synthesis without paired text-3D data","Existing CLIP-guided 3D optimization methods fail to generate accurate and faithful 3D structures that conform to input text due to lack of prior knowledge and random initialization","Mismatching problem between CLIP text and image embedding spaces in previous text-to-shape methods","Style domain gap between images synthesized by text-to-image diffusion models and shape renderings used to train image-to-shape generators"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to introduce explicit 3D shape priors into CLIP-guided 3D optimization for text-to-3D synthesis","Propose a direct bridging of text and image modalities for text-to-shape generation using a text-to-image diffusion model (Stable Diffusion) and an image-to-shape generator","Jointly optimize a learnable text prompt and fine-tune the text-to-image diffusion model to generate rendering-style images, reducing the style domain gap","Dream3D achieves superior visual quality and shape accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods in zero-shot text-to-3D synthesis"]}

methods: Introducing explicit 3D shape priors into CLIP-guided 3D optimization for text-to-3D synthesis

Two-stage pipeline: (1) text-to-shape generation using a text-to-image diffusion model (Stable Diffusion) followed by an image-to-shape generator, (2) using the generated 3D shape as initialization for neural radiance field optimization with the full text prompt
Joint optimization of a learnable text prompt and fine-tuning of the text-to-image diffusion model to generate rendering-style images and reduce the style domain gap
problems: Zero-shot text-to-3D synthesis without paired text-3D data
Existing CLIP-guided 3D optimization methods fail to generate accurate and faithful 3D structures that conform to input text due to lack of prior knowledge and random initialization
Mismatching problem between CLIP text and image embedding spaces in previous text-to-shape methods
Style domain gap between images synthesized by text-to-image diffusion models and shape renderings used to train image-to-shape generators
novelty_claims: First to introduce explicit 3D shape priors into CLIP-guided 3D optimization for text-to-3D synthesis
Propose a direct bridging of text and image modalities for text-to-shape generation using a text-to-image diffusion model (Stable Diffusion) and an image-to-shape generator
Jointly optimize a learnable text prompt and fine-tune the text-to-image diffusion model to generate rendering-style images, reducing the style domain gap
Dream3D achieves superior visual quality and shape accuracy compared to state-of-the-art methods in zero-shot text-to-3D synthesis

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["DINO: a self-supervised learning method for Vision Transformers (ViT) and convnets, based on self-distillation with no labels using a momentum encoder and multi-crop training","Use of momentum encoder for teacher network updates via exponential moving average (EMA)","Multi-crop training: using multiple random crops of images as augmentations","Use of small patch sizes in ViT architectures"],"problems":["Do self-supervised learning methods provide new properties to Vision Transformers (ViT) compared to convolutional networks (convnets)?","How to design a simple and effective self-supervised learning method for ViTs that leverages their unique properties?","How to achieve strong performance on standard benchmarks (e.g., ImageNet) with self-supervised ViTs?"],"datasets":["ImageNet"],"metrics":["Top-1 accuracy (linear evaluation on ImageNet)","Top-1 accuracy (k-NN classification on ImageNet)"],"results":[{"metric":"Top-1 accuracy (k-NN, small ViT, ImageNet)","value":"78.3%"},{"metric":"Top-1 accuracy (linear evaluation, ViT-Base, ImageNet)","value":"80.1%"},{"metric":"Top-1 accuracy (linear evaluation, ViT, ImageNet, 2x8 GPUs, 3 days)","value":"76.1%"}],"novelty_claims":["Self-supervised ViT features contain explicit information about semantic segmentation and object boundaries, which does not emerge as clearly with supervised ViTs or convnets.","Self-supervised ViT features perform exceptionally well with simple k-NN classifiers, achieving high top-1 accuracy without finetuning or data augmentation.","DINO is a simple and effective self-supervised method that works for both ViTs and convnets, requiring minimal architectural changes and no labels.","DINO achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results on ImageNet linear evaluation with ViT-Base and ResNet-50 architectures.","The combination of momentum encoder, multi-crop training, and small patch sizes is crucial for the emergence of these properties in self-supervised ViTs."]}

methods: DINO: a self-supervised learning method for Vision Transformers (ViT) and convnets, based on self-distillation with no labels using a momentum encoder and multi-crop training
Use of momentum encoder for teacher network updates via exponential moving average (EMA)
Multi-crop training: using multiple random crops of images as augmentations
Use of small patch sizes in ViT architectures
problems: Do self-supervised learning methods provide new properties to Vision Transformers (ViT) compared to convolutional networks (convnets)?
How to design a simple and effective self-supervised learning method for ViTs that leverages their unique properties?
How to achieve strong performance on standard benchmarks (e.g., ImageNet) with self-supervised ViTs?

results.metric: Top-1 accuracy (linear evaluation, ViT, ImageNet, 2x8 GPUs, 3 days)

novelty_claims: Self-supervised ViT features contain explicit information about semantic segmentation and object boundaries, which does not emerge as clearly with supervised ViTs or convnets.
Self-supervised ViT features perform exceptionally well with simple k-NN classifiers, achieving high top-1 accuracy without finetuning or data augmentation.
DINO is a simple and effective self-supervised method that works for both ViTs and convnets, requiring minimal architectural changes and no labels.
DINO achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results on ImageNet linear evaluation with ViT-Base and ResNet-50 architectures.
The combination of momentum encoder, multi-crop training, and small patch sizes is crucial for the emergence of these properties in self-supervised ViTs.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["DreamBooth: Fine-tuning a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model with a few images of a subject and text prompts containing a unique identifier and class name","Representing a subject with rare token identifiers to bind the subject to a unique word in the model's vocabulary","Autogenous class-specific prior preservation loss to prevent language drift and encourage diversity in generated images","Ablation studies to analyze the contribution of each component"],"problems":["Personalization of text-to-image diffusion models to synthesize novel images of a specific subject in new contexts","Inability of existing models to mimic the appearance of a given subject from a few reference images and generate new renditions in diverse scenes","Maintaining subject fidelity and prompt fidelity in generated images"],"datasets":["A new dataset of various subjects captured in different contexts (constructed and released by the authors for subject-driven generation)"],"metrics":["Subject fidelity","Prompt fidelity","User study (human evaluation)"],"results":[{"metric":"Subject fidelity (user study)","value":"DreamBooth outperforms alternative approaches in preserving subject identity (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt)"},{"metric":"Prompt fidelity (user study)","value":"DreamBooth achieves higher prompt fidelity compared to baselines (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt)"}],"novelty_claims":["First technique to enable subject-driven generation in text-to-image diffusion models using just a few images of a subject","Introduction of a new autogenous class-specific prior preservation loss to prevent language drift and encourage diversity","Ability to synthesize novel, photorealistic images of a specific subject in diverse scenes, poses, and lighting conditions while preserving key features","Creation and release of a new dataset and evaluation protocol for subject-driven generation"]}

methods: DreamBooth: Fine-tuning a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model with a few images of a subject and text prompts containing a unique identifier and class name
Representing a subject with rare token identifiers to bind the subject to a unique word in the model's vocabulary
Autogenous class-specific prior preservation loss to prevent language drift and encourage diversity in generated images
Ablation studies to analyze the contribution of each component
problems: Personalization of text-to-image diffusion models to synthesize novel images of a specific subject in new contexts
Inability of existing models to mimic the appearance of a given subject from a few reference images and generate new renditions in diverse scenes
Maintaining subject fidelity and prompt fidelity in generated images
datasets: A new dataset of various subjects captured in different contexts (constructed and released by the authors for subject-driven generation)

results.value: DreamBooth outperforms alternative approaches in preserving subject identity (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt)
DreamBooth achieves higher prompt fidelity compared to baselines (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: First technique to enable subject-driven generation in text-to-image diffusion models using just a few images of a subject
Introduction of a new autogenous class-specific prior preservation loss to prevent language drift and encourage diversity
Ability to synthesize novel, photorealistic images of a specific subject in diverse scenes, poses, and lighting conditions while preserving key features
Creation and release of a new dataset and evaluation protocol for subject-driven generation

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ConsistNet: a plug-in multi-view consistency block for latent diffusion models","Lightweight multi-view consistency block enabling information exchange across single-view diffusion processes","View aggregation module: unprojects multi-view features into global 3D volumes and infers consistency","Ray aggregation module: samples and aggregates 3D consistent features back to each view","Parallel diffusion models for each viewpoint with shared consistency enforcement","Plug-in architecture for pretrained latent diffusion models (LDMs), specifically Zero123-XL"],"problems":["Generating multi-view images of a 3D object from a single image while enforcing 3D consistency among views","Lack of mechanisms in standard latent diffusion models to ensure 3D consistency across generated views","Previous methods (e.g., Zero123) do not explicitly enforce multi-view geometry consistency"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["3D consistency (qualitative and quantitative)","Generation speed (time to generate multiple views)"],"results":[{"metric":"Number of views generated","value":"16"},{"metric":"Generation time for 16 views on single A100 GPU","value":"11 seconds"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of ConsistNet, a plug-in multi-view consistency block for latent diffusion models that enforces 3D consistency across generated images from different viewpoints","ConsistNet can be easily integrated into pretrained LDMs without requiring explicit pixel correspondences or depth prediction","Only the ConsistNet block is trained, while the base diffusion models remain frozen","ConsistNet surpasses previous methods (e.g., Zero123) in terms of 3D consistency and generalizes well to unseen data"]}

methods: ConsistNet: a plug-in multi-view consistency block for latent diffusion models
Lightweight multi-view consistency block enabling information exchange across single-view diffusion processes
View aggregation module: unprojects multi-view features into global 3D volumes and infers consistency
Ray aggregation module: samples and aggregates 3D consistent features back to each view
Parallel diffusion models for each viewpoint with shared consistency enforcement
Plug-in architecture for pretrained latent diffusion models (LDMs), specifically Zero123-XL
problems: Generating multi-view images of a 3D object from a single image while enforcing 3D consistency among views
Lack of mechanisms in standard latent diffusion models to ensure 3D consistency across generated views
Previous methods (e.g., Zero123) do not explicitly enforce multi-view geometry consistency
metrics: Generation speed (time to generate multiple views)

results.metric: Generation time for 16 views on single A100 GPU

novelty_claims: Introduction of ConsistNet, a plug-in multi-view consistency block for latent diffusion models that enforces 3D consistency across generated images from different viewpoints
ConsistNet can be easily integrated into pretrained LDMs without requiring explicit pixel correspondences or depth prediction
Only the ConsistNet block is trained, while the base diffusion models remain frozen
ConsistNet surpasses previous methods (e.g., Zero123) in terms of 3D consistency and generalizes well to unseen data
